latin: post septem et dimidium miliones annorum, posteri Fook et Lunkwill convenerunt ut Responsum audirent. 'salve,' tandem inquit Cogitatio Profunda. 'salve, O Cogitatio Profunda,' inquit Loonquawl trepidans, 'habesne... id est...' 'Responsum vobis?' interrupit Cogitatio Profunda magnifice. 'ita, habeo.' ambo tremuerunt. 'estne vere?' susurravit Phouchg. 'vere est,' confirmavit Cogitatio Profunda. 'ad Omnia? ad Magnam Quaestionem Vitae, Universi, et Omnium Rerum?' 'ita.' 'et parati estis id nobis dare?' 'paratus sum.' 'nunc?' 'nunc.' ambo labra sicca linxerunt. 'quamquam non puto,' addidit Cogitatio Profunda, 'vos id amaturos esse.' 'nihil refert!' clamavit Phouchg. 'dic nobis!' 'esto,' inquit Cogitatio Profunda. 'Responsum ad Magnam Quaestionem...' 'ita...!' 'Vitae, Universi, et Omnium Rerum...' 'ita...!' 'est...' 'ita...!' 'est...' 'ITA...?!' 'quadraginta duo,' inquit Cogitatio Profunda, cum infinita maiestate et tranquillitate.

questions.prompt: Who came to hear the Answer?
questions.target: The descendants of Fook and Lunkwill
choices: The descendants of Fook and Lunkwill

questions.prompt: What warning did Deep Thought give?
questions.target: They weren't going to like the answer
choices: They weren't going to like the answer

questions.prompt: What was the Answer to Life, the Universe, and Everything?
How did Deep Thought deliver the answer?

translation: After seven and a half million years, the descendants of Fook and Lunkwill gathered to hear the Answer. 'Good morning,' Deep Thought said at last. 'Good morning, O Deep Thought,' said Loonquawl nervously, 'do you have... that is...' 'An Answer for you?' Deep Thought interrupted majestically. 'Yes, I have.' They both trembled. 'There really is one?' whispered Phouchg. 'There really is,' confirmed Deep Thought. 'To Everything? To the Great Question of Life, the Universe, and Everything?' 'Yes.' 'And you're ready to give it to us?' 'I am.' 'Now?' 'Now.' They both licked their dry lips. 'Though I don't think,' added Deep Thought, 'that you're going to like it.' 'It doesn't matter!' cried Phouchg. 'Tell us!' 'All right,' said Deep Thought. 'The Answer to the Great Question...' 'Yes...!' 'Of Life, the Universe, and Everything...' 'Yes...!' 'Is...' 'Yes...!' 'Is...' 'YES...?!' 'Forty-two,' said Deep Thought, with infinite majesty and calm.
